intro: Caraguata devansayana, also known as Caraguata, is a type of plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Caraguata devansayana include annonaceous acetogenins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Caraguata is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea form, as well as being used in the production of ointments and creams for topical application.
